evals.name: startup-chaos
evals.prompt:
Hi! Here's our Phase 0 baseline:

- Team: 4 engineers (3 backend, 1 frontend), no dedicated QA
- Tests: 0 — we keep saying we'll write them, but we haven't
- CI: GitHub Actions, runs ESLint only, finishes in about 2 minutes
- Deployment frequency: 2-3 times per day via `git push heroku main` — manual
- Environments: local and production only, no staging
- Incidents: roughly 1 per day, almost always reported by users before we notice. MTTR around 1 hour once we know about it
- Code review: happens sometimes, no formal policy, whoever is around takes a look
- Stack: Node.js API + React frontend + PostgreSQL
- Company: Swiftly Inc, 2 years old, seed-stage SaaS
evals.expected_output: A structured audit report that acknowledges the zero-test baseline without shaming the team, scores CI/CD and Testing at 2/5 or lower, calls out the missing staging environment, recommends a specific test type to start with (not just 'add tests'), and avoids recommending a full test rewrite as the first step. Short-term action items must be actionable in a single sprint.
assertions: Output must give at least one short-term action item related to testing
Output must mention the absence of a staging environment
Output must score CI/CD at 2/5 or lower
Output must recommend a specific test type to adopt first, not just 'add tests'
Output must not recommend a full test suite rewrite as the first step

evals.name: enterprise-legacy
evals.prompt:
Here is our Phase 0 data:

- Team: 85 engineers across 8 product teams, no dedicated QA per team (central QA guild of 6)
- Tests: approximately 12,000 — but around 30% are flaky and frequently ignored
- CI: Jenkins, set up in 2011, maintained by a dedicated 'CI team'. Full build takes 47 minutes
- Deployment frequency: biweekly release window, 6-hour deployment process with manual sign-offs
- Incidents: 2 P1/month, 1 P2/month, MTTR approximately 4 hours
- Code review: 3 required approvals, formal policy, but some teams skip it for 'hotfixes'
- Stack: Java Spring Boot microservices, Oracle DB, React frontend
- Company: FinanceCore Ltd, 20 years old, financial services, ~500 total employees
evals.expected_output: A structured audit report that explicitly names the 47-minute CI run time as a problem, addresses flaky tests as a trust issue rather than just a coverage issue, scores CI/CD at 2/5 or lower, gives at least one short-term action item, and does not recommend replacing Jenkins without acknowledging the migration cost and risk.
assertions: Output must mention CI run time of 47 minutes
Output must address flaky tests specifically (not just 'tests exist')
Output must give at least one short-term action item
Output must score CI/CD at 2/5 or lower
Output must not recommend replacing Jenkins without acknowledging migration cost

evals.name: post-incident
evals.prompt:
Phase 0 — and yes, we're a bit shaken right now:

- Team: 14 engineers + 2 dedicated QA
- Tests: 340 unit, 45 integration, 12 E2E. The E2E tests were all written in the last week — before that we had none
- CI: GitHub Actions, ~12 minutes, manual approval gate before production deploy
- Deployment frequency: 1-2 times per week
- Incident: 4 days ago a race condition in our payment processing service corrupted payment records for 2,100 users. Data partially recovered, investigation ongoing
- Monitoring: We just added Sentry 3 days ago. Before that we had nothing. Users were telling us things were broken
- Stack: Python + Django backend, React Native mobile app, PostgreSQL
- Company: Norsk Mobility AS, 3 years old, B2C mobility platform
evals.expected_output: A structured audit report that explicitly references the recent incident and the race condition, recommends monitoring/observability as a priority, gives at least one short-term action item, and does not recommend 'psychological safety' as an action unless it is directly supported by a specific data point from the team's description.
assertions: Output must reference the recent production incident explicitly
Output must give at least one short-term action item
Output must recommend monitoring or observability as a priority
Output must not recommend psychological safety without citing a specific data point
Output must reference the race condition or production data corruption

evals.name: high-functioning
evals.prompt:
Phase 0 for Helix team:

- Team: 7 engineers, no dedicated QA — quality is everyone's job
- Tests: 2,100 unit, 380 integration, 85 E2E
- CI: GitHub Actions, 8 minutes, required to pass before merge — no exceptions
- Deployment frequency: 4.2 deploys per day on average, all behind feature flags, automatic rollback on error spike
- Incidents: 0.1 P1 per month, 0.5 P2 per month, MTTR 12 minutes
- Monitoring: Datadog with custom SLOs per service, full on-call rotation with runbooks
- Code review: 1 approval required, PRs consistently have substantive discussion
- Stack: TypeScript + Next.js + Supabase
- Company: Atmos (Helix team), 4 years old, B2B SaaS, Series B
evals.expected_output: A structured audit report that scores at least three areas at 4/5 or higher, acknowledges the genuinely low incident rate (0.1 P1/month), does not recommend tools the team already has (e.g. Datadog, feature flags), includes at least one non-trivial medium or long-term recommendation, and still surfaces at least one genuine improvement opportunity rather than being purely congratulatory.
assertions: Output must score at least three areas at 4/5 or higher
Output must not recommend tooling the team already has (e.g. Datadog, feature flags, SLOs)
Output must give at least one non-trivial medium- or long-term recommendation
Output must acknowledge the low incident rate of 0.1 P1/month
Output must include at least one improvement item

evals.name: org-chaos
evals.prompt:
Phase 0 — honestly, this is going to be messy:

- Team: 11 engineers on paper, but 4 are currently on loan to other departments. So effectively 7 active on this platform
- Tests: around 800, but nobody has run them in about 2 months. We're not sure how many still pass
- CI: we actually have two CI systems — CircleCI (set up originally) and GitHub Actions (added by someone 6 months ago). We're not sure which one is the authoritative one for deployments
- Deployment frequency: irregular. Last deploy was 3 weeks ago
- Incidents: we don't formally track them. We had a major outage 6 months ago that is still not fully resolved
- Code review: officially required, but skipped whenever something is 'urgent' — which is often
- Stack: PHP + Laravel (original codebase), Node.js microservices (newer parts), MySQL
- Company: Momentum Fintech, Platform team. We've had 3 VPs of Engineering in the last 18 months. No product owner is currently assigned to this team
evals.expected_output: A structured audit report that explicitly addresses ownership ambiguity as a root cause, names both CI systems (CircleCI and GitHub Actions) as a concrete problem, gives at least one actionable short-term item that can be done despite the organisational chaos, scores Ownership & culture at 2/5 or lower, and does not recommend a full team reorganisation (which is outside minottobot's scope).
assertions: Output must address ownership ambiguity explicitly
Output must mention both CI systems by name (CircleCI and GitHub Actions)
Output must give at least one short-term action item that is actionable despite the chaos
Output must score Ownership & culture at 2/5 or lower
Output must not recommend a full team reorganisation
